title: Kut From The Kloth Scrape Triage And Retrospective

metadata.purpose: Record the scrape triage session covering scrape rules, product-level scraping requirements, the Kut from the Kloth scrape, final metrics, and retrospective opportunities.

messages.text: Asked what was next in the scraping triage list and told Codex to proceed.

Asked Codex to update the scrape documentation so every scrape handoff reports rows with a distinct product URL, at least one measurement, a customer image, the ordered size, and the number qualified for Supabase insertion.
Specified that measurement extraction must use deterministic functions, not an LLM.

Updated the scrape rules so handoffs must include qualification metrics and so measurement and size extraction must be deterministic through provider fields, regexes, parsers, or other auditable code paths.
Asked for a report for the last scrape and questioned why the row count was low, specifically whether Codex had settled for an aggregate when product pages could have been scanned.

Confirmed that aggregate-only scraping is not acceptable as the normal path. Updated scrape instructions to require product-level scraping and to ask the human for product URLs if deterministic product URL discovery is blocked.
Asked for the next scrape. After discussion, approved proceeding with Kut from the Kloth.

Built `data-pipelines/non-amazon/scripts/step_1_raw_scrape/scrape_kutfromthekloth_reviews.py`, using Shopify `/products.json` for product discovery and Okendo product-level review endpoints for each product.

Fixed Okendo pagination by resolving relative `nextUrl` values against `https://api.okendo.io/v1`, fixed embedded image URL parsing, and tightened dedupe from review/image/product to review/image so grouped Okendo reviews did not duplicate rows across sibling product URLs.

Ran the full Kut from the Kloth scrape. Final output wrote 256 canonical review-image rows from 444 scanned products, with 187 distinct reviews, 256 distinct images, 54 distinct product URLs, 98 rows with measurements, 217 rows with ordered size, and 85 Supabase-qualified rows. The summary recorded `aggregate_only: false` and `measurement_extraction: deterministic_regex_and_provider_fields_only`.
Synced the resulting data to S3 with the existing data sync workflow.
Asked Codex to do a retrospective and decide whether anything could have been done to get more qualified rows.

Audited why rows failed qualification. Most non-qualified rows were missing measurements. Thirteen rows had measurements but no ordered size; seven of those looked recoverable with high-confidence deterministic text patterns such as explicit ordered, exchanged, bought, got, or wearing-size language, and three more had inline size phrases needing stricter review. Re-expanding grouped Okendo repeats would increase counts only by duplicating canonical rows and should not be used.
Asked Codex to update the transcripts table and relevant documentation for the work in this chat.

Created this focused transcript JSON, added `data-pipelines/non-amazon/docs/kutfromthekloth_scrape_2026-04-29.md`, and updated `new-scrape-rules.md` to require conservative deterministic ordered-size fallback checks and a low-qualified-row retrospective.
